name: Anna's water route — Running circular from Chella
name_original: La ruta del agua de Anna — Running circular desde Chella
region_intro: Jogging routes around Chella offer a diverse landscape characterized by ravines, natural springs, and gorges, providing varied terrain for runners. The region features notable natural sites like El Salto and the Fuente del Abrullador, which feeds into the Abrullador gorge. These natural features create a mix of scenic vistas and challenging paths, ideal for exploring the area's natural beauty. The municipality's focus on its natural sites makes it suitable for outdoor pursuits. Best jogging routes around Chella The most popular jogging route is Anna's water route — Running circular from Chella, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area's water sources and natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Viewpoint of the Salto de Chella loop from Bolbaite, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.7 km) path. This route offers views of El Salto, a prominent natural feature, and takes approximately 1 hour 30 minutes. Local runners also love the Viewpoint of the Salto de Chella – Bolbaite natural pool loop from Chella, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail leading through areas near the natural pool and El Salto viewpoint, often completed in about 55 minutes. Jogging around Chella is defined by its ravines, natural springs, and viewpoints, offering a blend of natural beauty and varied terrain. The network provides options for easy, moderate, and difficult running, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Chella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 13 reviews. More than 1100 runners have used komoot to explore Chella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Chella?
A: There are over 80 running routes around Chella, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy paths to more challenging trails.

Q: Are there easy running routes in Chella for beginners?
A: Yes, Chella offers several easy running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10349846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Chella&lt;/a&gt; is a relatively flat and shorter option, perfect for an easy outing.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Chella?
A: The running trails in Chella are characterized by a diverse landscape featuring ravines, natural springs, and gorges. You can expect varied terrain, from relatively flat paths near water sources to more undulating trails with moderate elevation changes, especially when exploring areas like the Abrullador gorge or viewpoints.

Q: Can I find running routes that feature waterfalls or natural pools in Chella?
A: Absolutely! Chella and its surroundings are known for their beautiful water features. You can explore routes that pass by natural pools and waterfalls. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33751069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Salto de Chella – Bolbaite natural pool loop from Chella&lt;/a&gt; takes you near a natural pool and offers views of El Salto. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vikings' Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaspar Waterfall and Pool&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running paths in Chella?
A: Many of Chella's easier and moderate routes are suitable for families, especially those that are shorter and have less significant elevation changes. The natural beauty and points of interest like natural springs and viewpoints can make for an engaging family outing. Always check the route details for distance and difficulty to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see along the running routes?
A: Chella's running routes offer numerous scenic spots. A prominent feature is El Salto, and you can enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5825175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salto de Chella Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts natural gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2893417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4070836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera de Anna&lt;/a&gt; lake, all of which can be incorporated into your running adventures.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Chella?
A: Yes, many of the running trails in Chella are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish at the same point without retracing your steps. A popular example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832597233/anna-s-water-route-running-circular-from-chella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anna's water route — Running circular from Chella&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the area's water sources and natural landscapes.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Chella?
A: The running trails in Chella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Runners often praise the diverse natural beauty, the varied terrain that keeps runs interesting, and the opportunity to explore features like ravines, springs, and viewpoints. The mix of easy, moderate, and difficult options also caters to a wide range of abilities.

Q: Are there longer running trails in Chella for endurance training?
A: For those seeking longer runs, Chella offers several moderate to difficult routes that extend for greater distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10350160" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Salto de Chella loop from Bolbaite&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is an 8.5-mile (13.7 km) path that provides a good endurance challenge with scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Chella?
A: Chella, located in the Valencian Community of Spain, generally experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are often considered the best times for running, with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winter offers cooler, pleasant conditions, though some trails might be damp after rain.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the running trails in Chella?
A: Chella is a municipality with local amenities. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within the town itself. These establishments provide convenient places to refuel or rest before or after your runs, especially if you're exploring trails that start or end near the town center.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Chella?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always recommended to check local regulations for specific trails or natural parks. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails if kept on a leash and owners clean up after them. Always be mindful of wildlife and other trail users.

Q: How accessible are the trailheads by public transport or car parking?
A: Access to trailheads in Chella typically involves a mix of options. While some routes might be accessible directly from the town center, others may require a short drive. Parking is generally available in or near the municipality. For specific public transport options to more remote trailheads, local information or a quick search for Chella's public transport links would be beneficial.
tips:
Viewpoint to the Salto de Chella with incredible views. A very nice area with access both on foot, by bicycle or by car. From here you can descend on foot or by bicycle to the foot of the waterfall. Worth visiting and appreciating!
** Viewpoint to the Salto de Chella with incredible views. A very nice area with access both on foot, by bicycle or by car. From here you can descend on foot or by bicycle to the base of the waterfall. Worth visiting and appreciating!
